timeline.reason: Processes in Group 1 indicate the execution of malicious code.
Processes in Group 2 suggest reconnaissance activities to identify system information.
Processes in Group 3 indicate the collection of data from the compromised system.
Processes in Group 5 show mechanisms being established for persistence on the system.
Processes in Group 6 suggest attempts to evade detection by security measures.
Processes in Group 7 indicate activities aimed at accessing user credentials.
Processes in Group 11 suggest the establishment of command and control channels.

analyst_summary: The attack began with the execution of malicious code, followed by discovery and collection of system data. Persistence mechanisms were established, and defense evasion techniques were employed. Credential access was achieved, leading to the establishment of command and control channels.
